qa_pairs.question: How can I determine which GPU model specifiers are available on a Slurm cluster, including Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) specifiers?
qa_pairs.answer: You can obtain a list of available GPU model specifiers from Slurm by using the command: `sinfo -o "%G"|grep gpu|sed 's/gpu://g'|sed 's/),/\n/g'|cut -d: -f1|sort|uniq`. This is especially useful if the documented table of available GPUs hasn't been updated with the latest changes. However, this command will not provide short synonyms for some MIG specifiers.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it important to always provide a specific GPU model specifier when submitting a Slurm job?
qa_pairs.answer: It is strongly recommended to always provide a specific GPU model specifier in your Slurm job scripts because if you don't, your job may either be rejected or sent to an arbitrary GPU instance. As very few programs can use an arbitrary GPU efficiently, specifying a model helps ensure your job runs successfully and optimizes resource allocation.

qa_pairs.question: What are Multi-Instance GPUs (MIGs)?
qa_pairs.answer: Multi-Instance GPUs (MIGs) are a technology that allows a single GPU to be partitioned into multiple isolated instances. This enables your jobs to potentially utilize a MIG instance, which is a portion of a GPU, rather than requiring an entire GPU.
